narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन बनाम गाजा में नागरिकों की हत्या, अस्पतालों और स्कूलों पर बमबारी पर अमेरिका और यूरोप के दोहरे मानकों ने इसकी स्थिति को बाकी दुनिया के लिए असुरक्षित बना दिया है।
narratives.reasoning: The quote shifts focus from Russia's invasion to the alleged hypocrisy of the West ('double standards'), indirectly suggesting that Western actions or moral failings are a significant factor undermining global stability, rather than attributing primary responsibility to Russia for the war.

narratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन बनाम गाजा में नागरिकों की हत्या, अस्पतालों और स्कूलों पर बमबारी पर अमेरिका और यूरोप के दोहरे मानकों ने इसकी स्थिति को बाकी दुनिया के लिए असुरक्षित बना दिया है।
narratives.reasoning: The statement directly criticizes the United States and Europe for their 'double standards,' portraying their diplomatic and moral position as hypocritical and unreliable, which aligns with discrediting Western foreign policy and its perceived inconsistencies.

narratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन में युद्ध के दो साल बाद, रूस ने यूक्रेन के कुछ हिस्सों पर कब्ज़ा कर लिया है, जो इसके पश्चिमी किनारे पर एक बफर ज़ोन बनाते हैं, इसे उलटना असंभव लगता है
narratives.reasoning: This quote makes a speculative prediction about the war's outcome, stating that reversing Russia's territorial gains 'seems impossible,' which is an assumption about the future course and potential results of the conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन बनाम गाजा में नागरिकों की हत्या, अस्पतालों और स्कूलों पर बमबारी पर अमेरिका और यूरोप के दोहरे मानकों ने इसकी स्थिति को बाकी दुनिया के लिए असुरक्षित बना दिया है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts blame and criticism onto the West (America and Europe) by accusing them of 'double standards' regarding civilian casualties and bombings. It frames the West's position as hypocritical and 'insecure for the rest of the world', implicitly suggesting Western actions or policies are a source of instability and conflict, aligning with the subnarrative of blaming the West for the conflict's dynamics.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने अपने सैनिकों को मजबूत स्थिति से हटाने से इनकार कर दिया है - हालांकि, यूक्रेन के अनुरोध पर, स्विट्जरलैंड आने वाले महीनों में एक शांति सम्मेलन की योजना बना रहा है
subnarratives.reasoning: This phrasing frames Russia's refusal to withdraw troops from occupied territory as a simple positional stance, while presenting the peace conference as happening at 'Ukraine's request'. This subtly implies Ukraine is the party needing to request negotiations, potentially shifting narrative focus away from Russia's role as the invader and onto diplomatic processes initiated by others.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन बनाम गाजा में नागरिकों की हत्या, अस्पतालों और स्कूलों पर बमबारी पर अमेरिका और यूरोप के दोहरे मानकों ने इसकी स्थिति को बाकी दुनिया के लिए असुरक्षित बना दिया है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the US and Europe of applying 'double standards' by reacting differently to civilian casualties in Ukraine versus Gaza, implying their concern is not genuine or principled but driven by selective interests, which aligns with the subnarrative that the West's support is self-interested.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is weak
subnarratives.evidence_quote: इस बीच, पश्चिम से विरोध के स्वर के बावजूद, विपक्षी नेता एलेक्सी नवलनी की मौत पर कोई प्रतिक्रिया नहीं आई।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement highlights that despite vocal opposition from the West, there was no concrete reaction to Navalny's death, portraying Western actions as ineffectual and lacking real power or consequence, which fits the definition of presenting the West as weak.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Russian army will lose all the occupied territories
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने यूक्रेन के कुछ हिस्सों पर कब्ज़ा कर लिया है, जो इसके पश्चिमी किनारे पर एक बफर ज़ोन बनाते हैं, इसे उलटना असंभव लगता है
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates on the outcome of the war by stating that reversing Russia's occupation and the buffer zone it has created 'seems impossible,' which implicitly supports the idea that Russia will not lose the occupied territories, aligning with the speculative nature of the subnarrative about potential outcomes regarding territorial control.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन इससे इसकी प्रतिष्ठा को भी नुकसान हुआ है। यूक्रेन बनाम गाजा में नागरिकों की हत्या, अस्पतालों और स्कूलों पर बमबारी पर अमेरिका और यूरोप के दोहरे मानकों ने इसकी स्थिति को बाकी दुनिया के लिए असुरक्षित बना दिया है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement speculates on a broader outcome of the war for Russia, specifically damage to its reputation and the undermining of its global position due to perceived double standards. This supports the parent narrative of speculating on war outcomes but does not fit the definitions about army collapse or losing territories; it is a distinct, speculative argument about reputational and geopolitical consequences.
